metadata.task_title: Capitalize the starting letter of every word in Word
metadata.instruction: Open Task ID 50.docx in Document > Task 50 folder, capitalize the first letter of every word in the title.
metadata.context: I am writing an article on Word and I want to modify the title format based on the guidelines.

l3.rationale: Hovering in the File Explorer home window.

l4.rationale: The user browses File Explorer from the Home view into Documents, opens the Task 50 folder, and launches Task ID 50.docx, which is navigation through the application to reach the file.

l5.rationale: The initial navigation, scrolling, selection, and item opening steps are all part of locating and opening Task ID 50.docx from the Task 50 folder.

l3.rationale: Hovering in the Word document text area.

l4.rationale: In Word, the user moves within the document title, deletes lowercase initials, and types capitalized letters to capitalize the first letter of each word in the title.

l5.rationale: This span contains the repeated cursor moves, deletions, and typing needed to rewrite the document title so each word starts with a capital letter.

l3.rationale: Hovering in the Word document after editing.

l4.rationale: After editing, the user hovers and clicks within the Word page to inspect the result without making further content changes.

l5.rationale: The cursor moves to the text without further edits, indicating a final inspection of the updated title formatting.

l3.rationale: Clicking the Computer Use Logger window switches focus to it.

l4.rationale: The final click switches focus from Word to the Computer Use Logger window, which is a window/application navigation action.

l5.rationale: The final window switch is a separate wrap-up action after reviewing the document.
